Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than December 30, 2021 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than December 30, 2021 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for February 17, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 18, 2022. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 4, 2022. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by March 25, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, March 30, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES via CourtCall:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and moving parties Thomas and Geraldine Mansfield.
Gayle Ketchie of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant Scott Technologies Inc.
Derek S. Johnson of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants ViacomCBS, Inc. and General Electric Company.
Michelle Golden of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendants Chevron Entities, Texaco and Union Oil of California.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling amended as follows: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Defendant Southern California Edison Company withdrew its opposition.

The trial date is April 4, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is April 15, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 30, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 18, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 1, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than, January 14, 2022. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
